_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize MI federal-House 2026 batch: thomas-j-smith-mi-08, brian-steven-jaye, christina-hines, eric-chung-mi-10, robert-lulgjuraj. All confirmed still running (Aug 4 2026 primary). Social-media protocol for challengers; null where no public position; never inferred from party.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-16: filed FEC H6MI08312 (active, 2026-05-17), Grand Blanc resident, on Aug 4 2026 MI-08 GOP primary ballot vs incumbent McDonald Rivet. Zero campaign website / social media / questionnaire / endorsement footprint and zero fundraising; no public policy positions located on any issue. Roster-verified, nothing scored (no fabrication).

thomas-j-smith-mi-08.notes_set: Thomas J. Smith (R) — confirmed active candidate, MI-08, Aug 4 2026 GOP primary (challenger to Rep. Kristen McDonald Rivet, D). Late, low-resource entry: FEC Statement of Candidacy 2026-05-17, no reported receipts. No campaign site, social media, Vote411, or single-issue endorsements found; no documented positions on any rubric issue. All cells null pending a published platform.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-16 via campaign site, 2022 progressive voters guide, FEC, endorsement pages. Conventional progressive Democrat; documented contrary positions on life, LGBTQ, immigration, guns. Scored from cited self-statements/endorsements; null where no public position.

brian-steven-jaye.notes_set: Brian Steven Jaye (D) — confirmed active, MI-10 open-seat Aug 4 2026 Democratic primary (5-way). FEC H2MI08154 (filed 2025-04-22, Democratic). Lawyer; 2022 Democratic nominee MI-09 (lost to McClain); Oakland County Dem Exec Committee. Documented progressive: pro-choice, pro-LGBTQ protections, 'National Democratic Gun Sense Candidate' (pro-gun-control), pro-refugee/asylum, M4A, no-tax-increase-under-$400k. No documented position on religious liberty, election integrity, foreign policy, or health-freedom items. Religion not stated.

0.note: Pro-choice; campaigns on reproductive rights / 'stronger protections' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice; does not advocate abortion abolition.

0.note: Pro-LGBTQ+; campaigns for 'stronger protections for the LGBTQ+ community' — contrary to affirming marriage as one man/one woman.

1.note: Supports LGBTQ+ legal protections; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

4.note: Supports promotion of LGBTQ+ protections in public policy — contrary to the cell.

1.note: Pro-refugee/asylum ('robust refugee resettlement'), endorsed by Voice for Refuge Action Fund — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

0.note: Recognized 'National Democratic Gun Sense Candidate'; backs 'common-sense gun regulations' — contrary to constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports gun regulations / addressing gun violence — contrary to opposing red-flag/mag-limit/AWB measures.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-16 via campaign site, Ballotpedia, EMILY's List + Giffords endorsements. Career violent-crime prosecutor; pro-choice, gun-control-endorsed Democrat. Tough-on-violent-crime profile noted but federal rubric has no public-justice category. Marriage/sexuality left null (no documented position).

christina-hines.notes_set: Christina Hines (D) — confirmed active, MI-10 open-seat Aug 4 2026 Democratic primary. Career prosecutor (Wayne Co.; Chief of SVU & Appeals, Washtenaw Co.); 2024 Democratic nominee for Macomb County Prosecutor (lost to R Pete Lucido). Self-identifies Christian. Endorsed by EMILY's List (abortion-rights bundler) and Giffords PAC (gun control); campaigns on 'reproductive rights' and 'voting rights.' Does not take corporate PAC money. No documented positions on marriage/sexuality, religious liberty, or border specifics. Federal tier does not score public-justice (her prosecutor record noted for the audit trail).

0.note: Pro-abortion-rights; EMILY's List endorsement + 'reproductive rights' platform — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice; does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List (an abortion-industry PAC whose model is bundled donations to endorsed candidates) — contrary to 'never accepted EMILY's List funding.'

0.note: Endorsed by Giffords PAC (gun control) — contrary to constitutional carry.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed gun-control candidate — contrary to opposing red-flag/mag-limit/AWB measures.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-16 via campaign priorities page, FEC, Victory Fund/Equality PAC/HRC endorsements. Progressive Democrat, fundraising frontrunner, openly LGBTQ+. Documented contrary on life and marriage; one documented cross-direction credit (explicit E-Verify support).

eric-chung-mi-10.notes_set: Eric Chung (D) — confirmed active, MI-10 open-seat Aug 4 2026 Democratic primary; fundraising frontrunner (FEC H6MI10292, ~$1.46M raised). Attorney (DOJ Civil Rights Div., Senate Judiciary, Commerce/CHIPS); Harvard/Yale Law; son of Vietnamese refugees, Sterling Heights. Openly LGBTQ+; endorsed by LGBTQ+ Victory Fund, Equality PAC, HRC. Pro-Roe. Notable cross-direction: explicitly supports E-Verify and 'securing the border' with a path to citizenship. No documented positions on guns, election integrity, religious liberty, or health-freedom items. Religion not stated.

0.note: Campaigns to 'restore the protections of Roe v. Wade'; criticizes Dobbs — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-rights; does not advocate abortion abolition.

0.note: Openly LGBTQ+; endorsed by LGBTQ+ Victory Fund — contrary to affirming marriage as one man/one woman.

1.note: Endorsed by Equality PAC; pro-LGBTQ-rights — contrary to opposing same-sex marriage.

2.note: HRC endorsement (explicitly pro-transgender-rights) — contrary to rejecting transgender ideology / affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Pro-LGBTQ-identity policy ('safe and free to live'), Victory Fund-endorsed — contrary to opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy.

1.note: Immigration enforcement 'focused on violent criminals, not law-abiding residents' + path to citizenship — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

3.note: Explicitly supports E-Verify (documented cross-direction position for a Democrat).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-16 via campaign issues pages, FEC, news. Confirmed on ballot after surviving a May 2026 signature/residency challenge. Documented conservative positions on budget, election integrity, border, and foreign-policy restraint; life, marriage, and guns left null (no public statements found — not inferred from party).

robert-lulgjuraj.notes_set: Robert 'Bobby' Lulgjuraj (R) — confirmed active, MI-10 open-seat Aug 4 2026 GOP primary (well-funded, FEC H6MI10326 ~$822K COH); survived a May 2026 signature/residency ballot challenge (state review found >1,000 valid signatures). Catholic; Albanian-American (grandparents fled communist religious persecution); former Wayne Co. & Macomb Co. assistant prosecutor; Federalist Society. Documented positions: Balanced Budget Amendment, photo voter ID + citizenship verification, restrict absentee voting, oppose drop boxes/ballot harvesting, finish the border wall, oppose amnesty, 'peace through strength / no more endless wars.' NO public position found on abortion, marriage/sexuality, or guns — left null (notable blanks for an R; not inferred).

2.note: Explicitly supports a Balanced Budget Amendment; warns on the ~$37T national debt.

1.note: Supports photo voter ID with citizenship verification (backs the 2025 federal EO).

2.note: Would restrict absentee voting to deployed military and the elderly — aligns with single-day in-person + narrow absentee exceptions.

3.note: Opposes 'drop boxes everywhere to enable illegal ballot harvesting'; cites Hamtramck harvesting.

0.note: Supports finishing the physical border wall and backing ICE/Border Patrol.

1.note: 'No more endless, senseless wars'; deploy troops only for vital American interests — restraint posture against forever wars.
